summary: Led by Mohamed Salah and Omar Marmoush under Hossam Hassan, Egypt fancy their chances of finally clearing the group stage (their last two appearances, 1990 and 2018, both ended there). A disciplined, transition-based side that held Spain to a goalless draw in March.
style: Drops into a 5-4-1 without possession using a man-marking system (the dropping midfielder can push up if needed). Works best in transition — feeding the pace of Salah and Marmoush or the 1v1 ability of Ibrahim Adel. In settled possession, quick switches to find the spare man and runs in behind.

counterReliance.note: Disciplined man-marking block, lethal breaking through Salah/Marmoush

strengths: Defensive discipline — held Spain 0-0 with the 5-4-1 man-marking block
Elite transition outlets in Salah and Marmoush
Ibrahim Adel's 1v1 threat to carry the break
weaknesses: Reliant on Salah for the biggest moments — Egypt tend to go only as far as he takes them
Limited sustained attacking control against deep blocks

keyPlayers.name: Mohamed Salah
keyPlayers.note: 33, after a rough final Liverpool season — but Egypt's talisman; scored both their goals at the 2018 WC, and four goals + an assist across his last five qualifiers

keyPlayers.name: Omar Marmoush
keyPlayers.note: 11 in 49 looks modest, but he's capable of the spectacular — this could be his breakout tournament

tactics: Defend deep and man-mark in a 5-4-1, then break fast through Salah and Marmoush; in possession, switch quickly to find space. They'll need Salah at his best to finally escape a World Cup group.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group G (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
